entries.title: What DOM Clobbering is
entries.body:
DOM Clobbering is a technique in which global variables are overwritten ("clobbered") by means of HTML elements with specific id or name attributes.
The browser automatically creates references to named elements on window and document, and these references shadow the values the script expects.
This breaks the logic of scripts and can lead to vulnerabilities when a script reads a "variable" that is actually substituted by our HTML.

entries.title: Where it applies - an HTML injection without scripts is needed
entries.body:
Exploitation requires any HTML injection on the page (not full-fledged JS).
The classic scenario: a filter/sanitizer strips <script> and event handlers but lets through "harmless" tags (a, form, input, iframe, base, img...).
The goal is to slip in elements with the right id/name so that the page's script reads a controlled value from them (a sink of the form x, x.y, x.y.value, etc.).

entries.title: Tools and resources
entries.body:
SoheilKhodayari/DOMClobbering (domclob.xyz) - a large list of DOM Clobbering payloads for different browsers (desktop/mobile).
yeswehack/Dom-Explorer - a web tool for testing various HTML parsers and sanitizers; it shows how the browser parses HTML and helps find mutation XSS.

entries.title: Practice labs (PortSwigger)
entries.body:
• Exploiting DOM clobbering to enable XSS
• Clobbering DOM attributes to bypass HTML filters
• DOM clobbering test case protected by CSP
Links: portswigger.net/web-security/dom-based/dom-clobbering/

entries.title: Clobbering x.y.value (form + output)
Clobbering x.y via id + name (DOM collection)
Clobbering x.y.z - 3 levels of nesting
Clobbering a.b.c.d - more than 3 levels (nested iframe srcdoc)
Clobbering x.username and x.password (URL parsing of href)
Clobbering forEach (Chrome only)
entries.body:
// Payload
<form id=x>
<input id=y name=z>
<input id=y>
</form>

// Sink
<script>x.y.forEach(element=>alert(element))</script>

entries.title: Clobbering a value via href (Firefox only)
Clobbering x.xyz via base href (Chrome only)
DOMPurify bypass via the cid: protocol (double quote is not encoded)

meta.tip: DOMPurify allows the cid: protocol, which does not encode the double quote ("). This lets you break out of the href attribute and smuggle onerror=alert(1) when the value is later inserted into the markup.
